title: Prosecution will not press charges in the new trial of the Hino case and the accused will be acquitted
summary: The prosecution changed its position in the new trial of the murder in Hino, Shiga Prefecture, and will not file charges, paving the way for the acquittal of the death row inmate.

title: Fire at a Tokyo elementary school leaves at least 10 injured
summary: A fire that started in the music room of an elementary school in Tokyo injured at least 10 people, including children, who were evacuated immediately.

title: Japan's Finance Minister warns about speculative actions against the yen
summary: Finance Minister Katayama warned that he will take 'decisive measures' against speculation that weakens the yen, which surpassed 161 yen per dollar.

title: Mexico beats South Korea and secures its place in the round of 16 of the World Cup
summary: The Mexican national team defeated South Korea and became the first team to qualify for the knockout stage of the World Cup, taking advantage of a Korean defensive error.

title: Japan's national team prepares in Monterrey for its second World Cup match without Kubo
summary: The Japanese national team traveled to Monterrey for its next match without the injured Takefusa Kubo, while tactical analyses highlight its defensive solidity.

title: US and Iran sign a 14-point memorandum to reduce tensions
summary: The negotiations between the United States and Iran resulted in a 14-point memorandum, as both sides measure their political and strategic gains.

title: Lotte to raise prices of 35 ice cream products in Japan
summary: Lotte will raise prices of 35 ice cream products, such as the popular 'Yukimi Daifuku', between July and October due to rising costs.

title: Two suspects arrested for defrauding 350 million yen in clinic transfer
summary: Police arrested two men accused of defrauding a company of 350 million yen with the promise of managing the sale of a medical business.

title: Emperor of Japan visits Leiden University during his trip to the Netherlands
summary: Emperor Akishino visited Leiden University, the oldest in the Netherlands, where he toured its facilities and admired works of art.

title: Ukraine launches massive drone attack on Moscow
summary: Ukraine carried out a massive drone attack on Moscow, while President Zelensky warned that the Russian capital 'will burn'.

title: Japan's CPI rises 1.4% year-on-year in May, same as previous month
summary: Japan's consumer price index rose 1.4% year-on-year in May, keeping pace with the previous month and below the 2% target.

title: Increase in bears and decrease in hunters raise risk of attacks in Japan
summary: Bear populations in Japan are growing and approaching urban areas, while the number of hunters is aging and decreasing, increasing the risk of incidents.

title: Canada thrashes Qatar 6-0 in World Cup football
summary: The Canadian national team achieved a resounding 6-0 victory over Qatar in the World Cup, taking advantage of their numerical superiority on the field.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi does not rule out meeting between his secretary and creator of fake video
summary: Prime Minister Takaichi did not deny the possibility that his secretary met online with the creator of a defamatory video against him.

title: Nikkei exceeds 71,000 points and marks a new all-time high
summary: The Nikkei index on the Tokyo Stock Exchange rose more than 800 points to exceed 71,900 yen, driven by optimism in the technology sector.

title: England beats Croatia in World Cup debut with an inspired Declan Rice
summary: England started the World Cup with a victory over Croatia, where Declan Rice was named MVP and Harry Kane showed he is in his best physical shape.

title: Neymar will miss Brazil's second World Cup match due to injury
summary: Brazilian star Neymar will not be available for the second World Cup match due to injury concerns, although he is already training with the team.

title: Japanese Diet approves reform to postal privatization law with injection of 65 billion yen
summary: The reform to the postal privatization law was approved by the Diet, guaranteeing an annual injection of 65 billion yen to Japan Post.

title: Barack Obama Presidential Center inaugurated in Chicago without Trump's presence
summary: The center that will preserve the legacy of former President Barack Obama opened its doors in Chicago in a ceremony to which Donald Trump was not invited.

title: Brain-computer interface allows ALS patient to communicate for two years
summary: A research team in the US enabled an ALS patient to communicate at home for two years using a brain implant.
